title: Earnings call: Vir Biotechnology reports Q1 2024 financials, pipeline progress
text: Vir Biotechnology reported Q1 2024 revenues of $56.4 million, down from $63 million in the same quarter last year. The company ended the quarter with $1.51 billion in cash and investments, though this represented a $118 million decline from the previous quarter. R&D expenses dropped to $100.1 million from $157.6 million year-over-year, primarily due to lower clinical development costs. SG&A expenses also decreased to $36.3 million from $46.8 million, reflecting disciplined cost management. CEO Marianne De Backer announced the departure of CFO Sung Lee and the nomination of two new independent directors: Dr. Norbert Bischofberger and Dr. Farid. The company also noted that founding board members Dr. Philip Sharp and Robert Perez will not seek reelection. The SOLSTICE trial completed enrollment ahead of schedule with 60+ participants across two cohorts. Early data showed rapid HDV RNA decline, with five out of six participants had undetectable HDV RNA. Updated results will be presented at the European Association for the Study of the Liver Congress on June 5. Hepatitis B trial data demonstrated that 26% of participants achieved hepatitis B surface antigen loss and 16% achieve functional cure when ellipsoran was combined with peginterferon alpha. The company reiterated its full-year 2024 financial guidance and emphasized its strong balance sheet for funding development priorities.
